D10 Imaging L1 Identify Atom

Exposure: Too Dark, Too Bright

underexposed crushes the shadows; overexposed clips the highlights — the histogram tells you which end you are losing

Exposure is how much light reaches the sensor. Two stops down halves it twice; two stops up doubles it twice. Underexposure crushes the shadows so nothing distinguishes a dark grey from black; overexposure pushes the highlights past the maximum so nothing distinguishes a bright grey from pure white.

Drag the exposure and watch both the image and the histogram move. A piled-up left edge is shadow clipping; a piled-up right edge is highlight clipping. A well-exposed shot fits its histogram inside the box without crashing into either wall.
